channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: China's US influence could face crackdown under slate of new bills
item.description: U.S. Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., rolled out several bills aimed at countering Chinese influence and ownership in the U.S. as tensions grow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their new minority and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate approves funding bill hours before shutdown deadline, sending to Trump for signature
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a Republican-led spending bill Friday hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ron Nessen, press secretary to President Ford, dies at 90
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ron Nessen, a veteran broadcast journalist who was press secretary for President Gerald Ford and sought to restore the integrity that the position had lost during the Nixon administration, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: DOUGLASS K. DANIEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules against advocates trying to help migrants sent to Guantanamo and to stop more transfers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Friday against immigration and civil rights advocates attempting to help migrants who had been sent to the Guantanamo Bay military base — and trying to prevent further transfers — days after the Trump administration transferred all migrants out of the facility
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz hits road to rip Trump policies as he works to keep his own national profile up
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz, the 2024 Democratic vice presidential nominee, held the first in a series of forums in Republican-held congressional districts outside of Minnesota. He is using them to dissect Trump administration actions and perhaps boost his own standing in the soulsearching Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: FBI investigating rise in swatting incidents after several conservatives targeted, Kash Patel says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel on Friday said that the agency is investigating the recent surge in swatting incidents on conservative media figures and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about the state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oz pledges to fight health care fraud but makes no commitments on Medicaid funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dr. Mehmet Oz promised senators on Friday to fight health care fraud and push to make Americans healthier if he becomes the next leader of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blasts Biden’s DOJ: They tried to turn US into a ‘corrupt communist' third world country
item.description: President Donald Trump routinely has blasted the Justice Department and the FBI for corruption since his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, New York Gov. Hochul discuss Constitution gas pipeline
item.description: Trump said a pipeline would lower the region's energy prices, but it remains uncertain how it could be approved.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: ICE arrests another individual who participated in anti-Israel Columbia protests
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers arrested a second person who participated in the anti-Israel protests at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another pro-Palestinian protester
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another person involved in the pro-Palestinian protests, escalating its crackdown on those engaged in demonstrations over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: The Democratic Party appears fractured as a torrent of frustration and anger has been unleashed on Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Matt Brown And Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk meets with head of National Security Agency to ensure it is aligned with Trump, spy agency says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk met this week with the leader of the National Security Agency, suggesting the nation's spy services won't escape the billionaire's scrutiny as he looks to slash spending and personnel across the federal government. Musk met Wednesday with Gen.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic Rep. Raúl Grijalva of Arizona, champion of environment and progressivism, dies at age 77
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic Rep. Raúl M. Grijalva, a champion of environmental protections and progressive ideals who took on principled but often futile causes during a two-decade career in Congress, died Thursday. Grijalva, who was 77, had risen to chair the U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump‘s steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: White House withdraws nomination of David Weldon to run the CDC
item.description: Dr. Dave Weldon, Trump's pick for director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, was withdrawn from consideration shortly before a scheduled Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tax
item.description: The European Commission is calling on President Donald Trump to revoke tariffs now that he's threatened a 200 percent tariff on European wine, Champagne and spirits if Europe goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs stoke anxiety with Minnesota officials over uncertain impacts
item.description: Minnesota U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar said she’s concerned about the consequences the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. She spoke at a press conference Wednesday that featured a Minnesota farmer who worries about the impacts on agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: In Rochester, a new program helps students’ families avoid eviction
item.description: In many cases, the cycle of homelessness can start with an eviction. Kids with unstable housing struggle to learn in school. So a new program in Rochester is preventing evictions from ever happening to kids and their families.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump is openly challenging U.S. allies by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25 percent as he vows to take back wealth he says was “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News
